Please solve this issue: Thesaurus iterator error handling swapped in IndexSnapshot

#### Description

When retrieving thesaurus keys from an IndexSnapshot, error handling logic appears to be inverted. The code sends thesaurus iterators to the results channel when there's an error, and sends errors when the operation succeeds.

#### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```go
// Create an index with thesaurus support
index, err := bleve.New("test.bleve", bleve.NewIndexMapping())
if err != nil {
    panic(err)
}

// Add some documents with thesaurus data
batch := index.NewBatch()
doc := map[string]interface{}{
    "content": "example text with synonyms",
}
batch.Index("doc1", doc)
index.Batch(batch)

// Try to get thesaurus keys - this should work but may fail due to error handling bug
snapshot, err := index.Advanced()
if err != nil {
    panic(err)
}

// This operation may not behave correctly due to swapped error handling
keys, err := snapshot.ThesaurusKeys("synonyms")
if err != nil {
    // Error might be returned when it shouldn't be
    fmt.Printf("Unexpected error: %v\n", err)
} else {
    fmt.Printf("Keys retrieved: %v\n", keys)
}
```

The issue occurs in the async segment processing where error and success cases appear to be handling results incorrectly.

[ASSISTANT]
Reasoning and analysis:
The reported bug says the async segment processing that returns thesaurus iterators has its error handling inverted: it sends iterators to the results channel on error and sends errors to the error channel when the operation succeeds. The fix is to swap those two sends so success sends the iterator to the keys/results channel and failure sends the error to the error channel. Below I show a minimal patch that corrects the channel sends, plus a suggested unit test you can add and commands to apply and run tests.

Patch (swap the sends so errors go to the error channel and iterators go to the keys/results channel):

```bash
# Create a patch file and apply it (adjust the file path to the actual file in your repo)
cat > fix-thesaurus-iterator-swap.patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: index_snapshot.go
@@
-                if err != nil {
-                    // previously inverted: sent iterator on error
-                    resp.keysChan <- iter
-                } else {
-                    // previously inverted: sent error on success
-                    resp.errChan <- err
-                }
+                if err != nil {
+                    // send the error back to the caller on failure
+                    resp.errChan <- err
+                } else {
+                    // send the iterator back to the caller on success
+                    resp.keysChan <- iter
+                }
*** End Patch
PATCH

git apply fix-thesaurus-iterator-swap.patch
```

If your repository file path differs (for example index_snapshot_thesaurus.go or snapshot_thesaurus.go), adjust the file name in the patch accordingly. The important change is the block that previously had the inverted sends: swap those two so errors are sent on the error channel and iterators on the keys channel.

Suggested unit test (add to your snapshot tests) to ensure correct behavior:

```go
func TestThesaurusKeys_ErrorHandling(t *testing.T) {
    // Create a snapshot/mock where segment processing returns an error
    // Expect the returned error to be non-nil and no keys returned.
    idx := setupTestIndexWithThesaurus(t)
    snap, err := idx.Advanced()
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatal(err)
    }

    keys, err := snap.ThesaurusKeys("nonexistent")
    if err == nil {
        t.Fatalf("expected error for missing thesaurus name, got keys: %v", keys)
    }

    // Now test successful case:
    // ensure thesaurus "synonyms" exists in the index
    keys, err = snap.ThesaurusKeys("synonyms")
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
    }
    if len(keys) == 0 {
        t.Fatalf("expected keys for thesaurus 'synonyms', got none")
    }
}
```

Run tests:

```bash
# run all tests in the module
go test ./...

# or run only the snapshot packages (adjust package path)
go test ./index -run TestThesaurusKeys_ErrorHandling -v
```

#### Description

When retrieving thesaurus keys from an IndexSnapshot, error handling logic appears to be inverted. The code sends thesaurus iterators to the results channel when there's an error, and sends errors when the operation succeeds.

#### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```go

}

// Try to get thesaurus keys - this should work but may fail due to error handling bug

if err != nil {
    panic(err)
}

// This operation may not behave correctly due to swapped error handling

    // Error might be returned when it shouldn't be

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: index_snapshot.go
@@
